entries.title: Welcome
entries.description: Flutter-first documentation starter for Arcane Jaspr projects
entries.excerpt: Welcome This template is set up to document a Flutter-first Arcane Jaspr project. Use it to teach the primary surface first, and keep advanced HTML or raw Jaspr APIs clearly separated. Start Here - $1 - $1 - $1 Documentation Rule Code samples in this template should read like normal Flutter-style Arcane Jaspr code, not like low-level HTML or raw Jaspr code.

entries.title: Introduction
entries.description: Primary documentation path for a Flutter-first Arcane Jaspr project
entries.excerpt: Introduction Use this docs template to document the primary Arcane Jaspr surface first. Primary Import Advanced Imports Keep advanced imports out of the default getting-started path unless the page is explicitly about those escape hatches. Same UI in Plain Jaspr Use a plain Jaspr example early in the docs when you want to explain the baseline that Arcane Jaspr is trying to improve on. Build With Arcane Jaspr Follow the plain Jaspr example with the Arcane version so readers can immediately see

entries.title: Installation
entries.description: Set up the docs site and align package examples with the primary Arcane Jaspr surface
entries.excerpt: Installation Set up the docs site first, then keep package examples aligned with the primary Arcane Jaspr import surface. Install Dependencies Run the Docs Site Build Static Output Package Example Rule Use this import in normal examples: Only add these when a page is explicitly about advanced escape hatches:

entries.title: Quick Start
entries.description: Example page content for a Flutter-first Arcane Jaspr docs site
entries.excerpt: Quick Start Use a simple counter app as the baseline example for the primary Arcane Jaspr surface. Keep examples on this surface unless the page is explicitly about advanced HTML wrappers or raw Jaspr escape hatches.

entries.title: Deployment
entries.excerpt: Deployment Deploy your static documentation site to various hosting providers. Firebase Hosting The template includes Firebase Hosting configuration. Setup 1. Install Firebase CLI: 2. Login to Firebase: 3. Initialize (already done in template): Deploy Build and deploy in one command: Or use the script: Other Providers Since the output is static HTML, you can deploy anywhere: Vercel Netlify GitHub Pages 1. Build the site 2. Copy contents to your branch 3. Enable GitHub Pages in repository
